[05:12] Um and what these things basically going to allow you to understand is where your body's heart health overall sits at.
[05:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: LDL Cholesterol (Low-Density Lipoprotein)
NÚMEROS: Acima de 100 (risco), acima de 125 (alto risco), 70-80 (ideal)
VISUAL: Escala de risco com zonas coloridas, ilustração de artérias com placa aterosclerótica, setas indicando níveis perigosos vs. desejáveis
[05:25] So LDL cholesterol is one of the main ones that I want you guys to pay attention to. So that stands for uh low lipo dental protein. Okay?
[05:35] And that is essentially a a cholesterol that builds up in your arteries and causes osteiocerosis. So that is what causes heart attack, strokes, and a leading contributor to heart disease.
[05:48] Anything above 100 means you're fucked. Okay? So if you see it on the scale and it says it's above 100, that means it's way too high. Anything above 125, you're extremely at risk.
[06:00] The desirable range for this number is between 70 and 80. Okay? So if you just think about it like data, I need to get my number down to 70 and 80. That's all that means.
[06:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Strategies to Lower LDL Cholesterol
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Três estratégias com ícones: 1) Aumentar fibras (aveia, quinoa, lentilhas), 2) Aumentar ômega-3 (peixes de água fria, abacate), 3) Diminuir gordura saturada (fast food, cortes gordurosos)
[06:15] Strategy you can do to to get that number there is to increase the amount of fiber in your diet. So that's like oatmeals, that's like quinoa, that's like lentils. Okay?
[06:25] Increase the amount of omega-3s in your diet. That's like cold water fishes, that's like avocado. Um, and decrease the amount of saturated fat. That's that shitty ass McDonald's back there. That's like fatty cuts of pork, fatty cuts of beef.
[06:38] Okay? So simply put, decrease saturated fats, increase omega-3s, increase fiber. Okay? that will lower LDL cholesterol, increase HDL cholesterol, which is typically thought of like the good cholesterol.
[06:52] That's kind of like a snow plow, right? Like you guys who live in colder parts of the country, it's going to go through your arteries and clean out all the bad shit.
[07:02] So, you want to increase HDL, you want to decrease LDL. Okay.

[07:10] Another panel that we need to look at is a metabolic panel, comprehensive metabolic panel. This is going to look at your blood sugar.
[07:18] So, there's an aspect of this of looking at it, okay, for for health and preventive reasons, saying like, okay, I want to make sure that my blood sugar high is because I don't want to be pre-diabetic and diabetic.
[07:30] But the main reason is for performance. If your body is insulin resistant, meaning the cells in your body does not absorb glucose properly, you are going to suffer from fatigue way faster.
[07:43] You're not going to be able to cognitively function as fast and you're not going to be able to perform all your daily tasks at an elite level.
[07:52] So, what does that mean for you guys?
[07:55] Yes, exactly. So if your body is not being able to tolerate the food that you put into your body on a daily basis at its most optimal level, you're going to feel like shit.
[08:07] You're not going to perform your best. Your cognitive function is going to be going around 70 to 80%. So you literally be leaving money on the table if your body is not insulin sensitive.

NÚMEROS: 70-80% (função cognitiva reduzida com resistência)

[08:25] Okay? So understanding what the difference between insulin sensitive insulin resistant, you want to be more sensitive. All that essentially means is that your body is going to eat the is going to utilize the food properly that you that you put into your body.
[08:40] And so a comprehensive metabolic panel is going to look at a glucose panel. Again, anything above 100 on this marker, you're fucked. Anything above 125, oh, you're really fucked.
[08:52] Okay, what you want to be here is about 70 to 80 on this scale. Same too. That's a desirable range for this number too as well.

[09:38] but what people don't tell you is like if you just look at testosterone you say like okay I'm around 7 or 800 is that with inside the normal range okay cool that doesn't mean anything

[10:03] So, with your testosterone, you need to look at what's called your free testosterone. Your free testosterone is what your body actually has available for you to use for anabolic activity, for focus, move, drive, sex drive, libido, all that kind of stuff.
[10:20] So, it's not just enough to understand what your total testosterone is. That's just like bragging. That's like saying contracts created. That's not cash in the bank. You need to understand what your body is actually utilizing. Does that make sense?

[24:35] Same thing like with body weight. How many times you track your body weight per week, Cody? Every morning.
[24:45] Yeah. Why do you think I have him track his body weight every single day? Exactly. You can get really lost if you see high and lows. think you're doing good or you think you're doing bad.
[24:58] But if you get a 7-day weekly average, you compare it to the following average of the next week, you know exactly where you're going. You follow the trend.
[25:10] So, you don't get caught up in the highs and lows because they don't really matter. It's like you have a banging day of ads and you see like the shit's going off popping. Oh, I'm I'm I'm about to be rich.
[25:25] Like, no, that's just one fucking day cuz the next two or three days could be really off, but it's just following the trends to follow data over time. Does that make sense?
[25:38] Yeah. So, you got to take your emotions out of it and just literally just look at it like the same way you look at your business. The same exact way.

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
73% dos americanos estão com sobrepeso ou obesos
800,000 mortes/ano por doença cardíaca
1 em 4 mortes causadas por doença cardíaca (principal causa de morte em homens)
1 em 3 americanos são pré-diabéticos ou diabéticos
42% dos americanos são deficientes em vitamina D
LDL ideal: 70-80 (acima de 100 = risco, acima de 125 = alto risco)
Glicose ideal: 70-80 (acima de 100 = problema, acima de 125 = alto risco)
Testosterona total: 250-1100 ng/dL (range normal)
Testosterona livre: 42-224 (ideal próximo a 224)
1% de desidratação = impacto significativo na cognição
70-80% de função cognitiva quando resistente à insulina
168 horas/semana - apenas 3 horas (menos de 2%) dedicadas à saúde = ROI exponencial
JC: 545 lbs bench press, 5 recordes de powerlifting, virou pro WBFF aos 22 anos
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Blood Work (2-4x/ano):
Painel Lipídico (LDL, HDL, colesterol total)
Painel Metabólico Abrangente (glicose, função renal)
Hormônios (testosterona total, livre, SHBG)
Vitamina D
CBC (hemograma completo)
Painel mínimo: 64 pontos (CMP, CBC, testosterona, SHBG, lipídico, TSH)
Suplementação Estratégica:
Boron: 6 mg/dia (aumenta testosterona livre, absorção vitamina D, +34% boost)
D-Aspartic Acid: 3.2g/dia (libera testosterona)
Vitamina D: 5,000 IU + K2 + Boron
Creatina Monohydrate (CreaPure): 3-5g/dia (sempre)
Beta Alanine: 1.6-3.2g/dia (atrasa fadiga)
Citrulline Malate: 4-8g/dia (pumps, disfunção erétil)
Alpha GPC: 100mg (pré-treino)
Berberine: para controle de açúcar no sangue
Sal: para retenção de hidratação
Nutrição:
Proteína: mínimo 20g/refeição, 0.8-1.2g/lb massa magra
Mulheres: 100g/dia mínimo
Hidratação: 100+ oz/dia (garrafa 80oz da Amazon)
Estratégia carboidratos: empilhar 1-2h antes de dormir para serotonina/sono
Eliminar carboidratos diurnos se resistente à insulina
Fibras + Ômega-3 + Reduzir gordura saturada = baixar LDL
Caminhada 5-10 min pós-refeição = efeito Metformin
Sleep Optimization:
Sem luz azul 1h antes
Carboidratos complexos 1-2h antes (batata doce, arroz integral, quinoa)
Cortar água 1h antes + salt tabs (evitar urinar à noite)
Tracking diário peso corporal → média semanal (elimina emoção)
Movimento:
3 horas/semana dedicadas à saúde (menos de 2% do tempo)
Treino força: essencial para densidade óssea
Treadmill desk para trabalho
Cold/hot therapy para recuperação
ESTRUTURA:
Ciclo Vicioso Identificado:
Baixa Vitamina D → Mau humor
Mau humor → Fome aumentada por carboidratos/açúcar/gordura
Má alimentação → Resistência à insulina
Resistência insulina → Fadiga + baixa cognição (70-80%)
Fadiga → Excesso cafeína → Desidratação
Desidratação → Mais fadiga
Repete o ciclo
Kinetic Chain:
Tudo no corpo está conectado
Consertar um problema (ex: vitamina D) corrige cadeia inteira
Não tratar sintomas isoladamente (band-aid over bullet wound)
Dados > Emoções (mesma mentalidade de negócios)
Case Study - Julian:
Antes: 1g cafeína/dia, crash 4h às 13h, trabalho ineficiente 17h-meia-noite, 4h sono, insônia, farmácia de pílulas
Intervenção: Proteína diurna, zero carboidratos dia, big carb meal pré-sono
Resultado: Virada em 1 DIA - sono restaurador, ritmo circadiano corrigido
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
T3 Body Program:
Investimento: $4,000-$6,000
Duração: 6-12 meses
Inclui: Blood work completo, screening biomecânico, biofeedback semanal, planos treino personalizados, ajustes em tempo real (lesões, viagens)
ROI: "Recupera em 1 dia/semana uma vez otimizado"
Blood Work Costs:
Através de JC: ~$300
Direto/sem desconto: $1,000+
Risco seguro: pode custar $9,000 se classificado como "exploratory"
Meal Prep:
Trifecta: serviço delivery
Private Chef (CA): ~$2,000/mês
Quest protein powder: para cozimento
Supplements Budget:
Boron: $9
Creatine: ~$9-15
Maioria suplementos mencionados: baixo custo
Pre-workout comercial: desperdício (proprietary blends)
DIY pre-workout: mais barato e efetivo
Verdades Desmistificadas:
Comer colesterol ≠ aumentar colesterol sanguíneo
Carboidratos antes de dormir ≠ ganho de peso
Anabolic window = bro science
Máximo proteína/hora = bro science
Dietas por tipo sanguíneo = completo BS
Colágeno: bom para articulações, NÃO para pele/cabelo/unhas
Neutropics: band-aid (exceto Lion's Mane com buildup)
Alkaline water: desperdício
Loading phase creatina: desnecessário
Timing creatina: irrelevante (apenas 1x/dia)
Filosofia Central: "Você não pode colocar band-aid em ferimento de bala" - tratar causa raiz, não sintomas. Mesma mentalidade data-driven de negócios aplicada à saúde = deixar dinheiro na mesa se não otimizar.
DURAÇÃO: ~1h 38min FORMATO: Apresentação educacional com Q&A extensivo em evento mastermind
